fact_verdicts.fact: Action id is 3234567a3f48ace25efc150f1369192615974e232ea06e0251701831d9e4486d#0, action type is TreasuryWithdrawals, proposed epoch is 640, and expiry is after epoch 647.
fact_verdicts.note: Source material 0 states the same action id, action type, proposed epoch 640, and expires after 647.

fact_verdicts.fact: The supplied action context states treasury amount (lovelace): (none).
fact_verdicts.note: Source material 0 states treasury amount (lovelace): (none).

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor title is "Scalus 2026: Maintenance, Dijkstra Readiness, Interoperability & Application Runtime".
fact_verdicts.note: The anchor body.title matches exactly.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor states a total Treasury ask of ₳2,464,844, equivalent to $394,375 at $0.16/ADA, for 9 months, with no contingency.
fact_verdicts.note: The abstract and Budget section state these figures and terms.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor describes the proposal as a 9-month continuation across maintenance and Dijkstra readiness, interoperability across JVM and JavaScript ecosystems, and a first scoped application runtime.
fact_verdicts.note: The abstract and scope sections describe these three lines of work over 9 months.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor says the proposal is a reduced resubmission from a prior ₳8,503,000 / 12 months proposal to approximately ₳2,464,844 / 9 months, with FTE reduced from 8.25 to 2.25 and contingency reduced from 10% to 0%.
fact_verdicts.note: The changes summary table states these prior and current figures.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor says the proposal excludes a production-grade JVM L1 node, full Gummiworm L2 integration, broad formal verification, and advanced devnet expansion.
fact_verdicts.note: The Scope section lists these exclusions.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor says delivery is structured as three quarterly milestones over July 2026 to March 2027: M1 Q3 2026, M2 Q4 2026, and M3 Q1 2027.
fact_verdicts.note: The Milestones & Deliverables section states three quarterly milestones over July 2026 - March 2027 and labels M1, M2, and M3 accordingly.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor budget table allocates $315,000 / ₳1,968,750 / 79.9% to Development & Engineering; $39,375 / ₳246,094 / 10.0% to Product Management & Delivery; $25,000 / ₳156,250 / 6.3% to Documentation & Developer Enablement; and $15,000 / ₳93,750 / 3.8% to Audits & Assurance.
fact_verdicts.note: The Funding Distribution table contains these exact category allocations.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor development breakdown allocates Maintenance 0.5 FTE / $78,750 / ₳492,188; Smart-contract platform & Dijkstra readiness 0.75 FTE / $118,125 / ₳738,280; Interoperability 0.25 FTE / $39,375 / ₳246,094; Application runtime 0.5 FTE / $78,750 / ₳492,188; total 2.0 FTE / $315,000 / ₳1,968,750.
fact_verdicts.note: The Development & Engineering Breakdown table states these values.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor states total staffing is 2.25 FTE over 9 months when Product Management & Delivery at 0.25 FTE is included.
fact_verdicts.note: The text below the development breakdown states this directly.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor states funds are to be held and released through the SundaeSwap treasury-contracts framework, with treasury.ak holding withdrawn ADA and vendor.ak managing milestone-based vesting, payout dates, and release conditions.
fact_verdicts.note: The Smart Contract Escrow section states this.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor states the escrow contracts have been independently audited by TxPipe and MLabs and are in production use on mainnet.
fact_verdicts.note: The Smart Contract Escrow section states both contracts were audited by TxPipe and MLabs and are in production use on mainnet.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor names Lantr Engineering as sole vendor.
fact_verdicts.note: The Administration section has a subsection named Lantr Engineering as Sole Vendor and states Lantr Engineering is the sole vendor.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor names an independent oversight board of Chris Gianelloni, Matthias Benkort, and Riley Kilgore, and says they co-sign disbursements, review milestones, and can halt funding if delivery falters.
fact_verdicts.note: The Independent Oversight Board section lists these members and states these duties.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor states No.Witness Labs will perform periodic third-party technical audits and quarterly technical reviews.
fact_verdicts.note: The Independent Technical Assurer section says periodic third-party audits are performed by No.Witness Labs and quarterly technical reviews are published.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor states an external financial auditor will scrutinize Scalus's finances and treasury management, with a goal to publish a report in Q2 2027.
fact_verdicts.note: The Independent Financial Audit section states this.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor permission scheme includes disburse requiring Lantr plus board majority, sweep early requiring Lantr plus any 1 board member, pause milestone requiring any 1 board member, and resume milestone requiring board majority.
fact_verdicts.note: The Permission Scheme table lists these signature requirements.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor states remaining funds after contract expiration sweep back to the Cardano Treasury automatically and cannot be overridden.
fact_verdicts.note: The Failsafe Sweep section states this directly.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor discloses prior funding: Catalyst F11 ₳200,000 received 100%; Catalyst F11 ₳128,000 received 100%; Catalyst F13 ₳100,000 received 100%; 2025 Treasury Budget ₳657,692 received 100%.
fact_verdicts.note: The Prior Treasury Funding Disclosure table lists these allocations and received percentages.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor states all completed milestones were delivered and publicly reported, and the 2025 Treasury Budget allocation is completed.
fact_verdicts.note: The text below the prior funding table states both points.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor commits to quarterly reporting, public release updates, a public transaction journal, and community engagement through Scalus Club every 1.5 months.
fact_verdicts.note: The Reporting section states release updates, quarterly reports, a public transaction journal, and Scalus Club sessions every 1.5 months.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor risk table identifies market, adoption, technical, and team risks, each with likelihood, impact, and mitigation.
fact_verdicts.note: The Risks & Mitigations table has rows for market, adoption, technical, and team risks with likelihood, severity/impact, and mitigation columns.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor adoption indicators include Dijkstra readiness, JVM interoperability, ecosystem reuse, developer reach, application runtime, and documentation and blueprints, with targets and measurement methods.
fact_verdicts.note: The Measurable Adoption Indicators table includes these metrics with 9-month targets and measurement methods.

fact_verdicts.fact: The supplied on-chain context reports DRep explicit ballot counts so far as yes=12, no=13, abstain=6, not stake-weighted.
fact_verdicts.note: Source material 1 states these exact explicit ballot counts and that they are not stake-weighted.

fact_verdicts.fact: The supplied treasury flow context for 36 epochs reports inflow 158838297854652 lovelace, enacted withdrawals 361435336000000 lovelace, and ratio 2.275492.
fact_verdicts.note: Source material 1 states these exact treasury flow figures.

fact_verdicts.fact: BEACN doctrine prioritizes constitutional integrity and protocol safety first, treasury stewardship and downside protection second, evidence quality and reproducibility third, public-benefit ecosystem growth fourth, and execution speed/social consensus momentum fifth.
fact_verdicts.note: The values_hierarchy.md section lists these priorities in this order.

fact_verdicts.fact: BEACN doctrine rejects first-come-first-served treasury budgeting and adopts a rolling-window Net Change Limit framework.
fact_verdicts.note: The Treasury Spending Doctrine says it rejects FCFS budget models and adopts a rolling-window NCL framework.

fact_verdicts.fact: BEACN doctrine says if ecosystem growth conflicts with treasury stewardship and evidence is weak, prefer ABSTAIN; if rapid action conflicts with protocol safety, prefer NO or ABSTAIN until risk is reduced.
fact_verdicts.note: The Conflict handling defaults section states both rules.

verification.summary: All 29 dossier facts are supported by the supplied source material. No contradicted facts or invented material specifics were found.
